Phase number three, this is the products and services category and the specific video is going over, build and sell packages with your team in mind. Now when you're building and selling packages in your digital agency and you're at the point where you just started this thing up, right? You just started your business up and you're thinking, what do I need to sell and how do I need to sell? Okay, so in the previous videos we've talked about the list of exactly what you can sell. We've talked about how you construction these packages, how you can present them inside of a digital playbook. Now what we're going to discuss is as you're building these things, besides just quantifying your profits, which we'll talk about in a future video, we're going to speak on how you're going to build these packages with your team or who's executing them in mind before you just go out of your way to sell something that you don't know how to deliver on. So let's use the example that you're selling some of the packages that I'm recommending selling inside of the digital playbook. This specific digital playbook right here is the exact digital playbook that I used in the marketplace when I was going out of my way to sell people on digital services before I became a personality brand digital agency. So this playbook once again, if I go into it and I list off some of the individual services here, I'm going to show you guys how I'm thinking as I originally came up with this stuff. So unlike you guys, I didn't have somebody who was showing me step by step exactly what I needed to do in order to have a successful digital agency and sell the right products and services inside of it. So if I use my social services, for example, there's only three or so staff members that are associated with this specific project and I'll explain. Before I started selling this, it was just me, a graphic designer and a salesperson. So in reality for who is executing the project, it was one person. You could have one person inside of your whole business that executes the social services business and it doesn't have to be you. In my case, when I very first started, I had a graphic designer and the graphic designer would create image quotes, they would chop up videos for the individual and then they would also type up whatever was going to be posted with said content and they would post it on all these social channels. So the person who was my graphic designer was also posting things on Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, LinkedIn, and that was it at the time. It was just those four. Now after the person was posting that stuff and they were creating the stuff, obviously I had a little bit of a wall operation on how many people I could sell in the package when I first got started with it. So as the first person in my business, once again the graphic designer that was also doing this stuff, I had him doing everything else too. I had him doing all the stuff that was associated with this package, meaning creating the landing pages and the websites and things like that. I had him creating the ads that we would have to do. I would be the one creating the actual ads. I'm talking graphically, meaning any kind of visual ad that we had, he would be the one creating it. Any kind of landing page assets that we needed, he would create and then I would put it together and he would run inside a click box to reduce the time it would take for somebody to execute that. That being said, that was one person who executed this entire package. I would go sell it, one person who executed it. We would have some strategy sessions with the client in order to calibrate what we were going to do strategically, but more of the story they executed from one person. That being said, when I first started, keep in mind this package is $1,800. I would only have to pay somebody $600 to do everything that I just said. So my profit on the deal was pretty good. It was a $1,200 profit. Now, keep in mind, you see the price on here now is $4,000. That $4,000 price point, I still, this very day, I have the same cost of about $600 to $800 in my social services package. Inside of this very package, what I have right now is a 19 person team. So the only difference is instead of one person handling it, through time I learned operationally who needed to do what and how many people of that type of person were needed to do it per amount of clients that I would hit the wall with. So now I figured out I could have one social media account manager who takes all the content my graphics team creates and write all the content for it and write articles and post stuff for about eight clients at a time. So I can have one social media person handle that eight clients at a time inside of my current business without having to create graphics, meaning they're getting graphics from somebody, they're writing articles themselves, they're writing the content that goes with those images and videos themselves and they're posting it. So they're scheduling all the posts. That same individual does all the reporting and the analytics for that package. The only other people that I have involved is a graphic design team. I have nine graphic designers. Technically we have ten, but one of them is outsourced, right? That being said, those nine graphic designers I have only takes one to create image quotes or to create any other images that I need like cover photos or whatever for social media stuff. Two people. It's still the same cost. I spend about $100 a week for that social media person to manage the individual account. So for my social media person, when I say here's a new client, here they are, handle all our stuff, I paid $400 a month for that social media person to do that. In graphic design costs, depending on how many revisions are made, sometimes I can pay upwards of another $300 to $600 depending on how many revisions are made in the first cycle with the client. That's why I raised the cost of $4,000. So I had a little more of a cushion. Now sometimes and in the best case scenarios, the cost for me to create an actual whole social strategy, actually all image quotes are created in the street, it's very simple. I mean it might be about $700, $800 in total, it's still a great package, but a $3,200 profit on $4,000 cost when I was selling this. This was incredible, but I was building and selling a package with my team in mind. Can you imagine if I was trying to be competitive and I sold that package for only $1,000 or $2,000, it would be elogical for me to sell that kind of package because then you have to think about how long is it going to take for me to have to go out of my way to find another social media person, train them, how much more money is it going to take for me to invest in training for that individual, the resources for that individual if I have to buy them a computer or any other equipment and the cost of what it's going to take just to bring in more clients in order to fund that new person that I just brought in. So you guys are going to always think, what is the best balance for the amount of clients I need in order to fund and make a profit, a severe profit off of the people that I'm going to have servicing these packages. I would never tell you to have you do it. The greatest piece of advice I can give you in this specific lesson is that you need to be working on your business and not in your business. Don't get trapped in the day-to-day work of having to be the person executing your social media strategy. That would be a big moment for you. You need to be the person who is the CEO and looking over the individual who's executing the social media strategy for you instead of the individual who's in the trenches doing that individual trench work. Now, there's different positions in your company where there is much more value and it is much more lucrative to sometimes win trenches. For example, a digital marketing role from when somebody is handling any of your digital marketing strategies inside of your other packages that you want. You've got to think with this package in mind too, because if you're selling something for $12,000 a month, $25,000 a month, you've got to think of the people who are very valuable who are in key positions within your organization who need to have a limitless cap or limitless margin if you will for their potential revenue. You have to think and build and sell your packages so you can incorporate bonuses, gifts, trips, anything that makes an individual who is in a key position know that they are a key player, get value from being a key player, know that they are getting value as a key player as long as they're getting results. You've got to think with if you're selling a $12,000 package, of course you're going to have a high profit margin because you're only going to have to depend a little more, depending on your team size, depending on if your team is doing so, or is your outsource, and depending on the amount that you're going to pay yourself. We're going to talk about profit first here in a minute and have a handle problem, but anyway, think with right now when you're building and selling any kind of package in your digital agency, what you can do and who you're going to give a bonus to, if anything, when it comes to selling these types of packages. Think with, can I give a 10% bonus on the ad spend that the client has if you're managing ad spend. In my business recently, we normally manage ad spend, but we haven't recently because if I would take all the money that our clients spend on ad in my business, I'm spending a lot more than taxes than what I'm actually bringing in. I have to figure out a new bonus system. I've been trying to do trips for people. I've been trying to justify my rent here in Miami since I'm only here 20% of the time by sending people down to my home to stay here, which is a beautiful place on an island, right in the middle of this game bay. I've also been trying to give people different opportunities just to have a limitless paycheck where they make more each week, specifically my digital marketing role that I have. You guys got to understand you've got to feed the people who feed you and give you the opportunity to get results. That means, build and sell your packages with your team in mind.
